title: Using a Grid Map to Find Distance

steps.instruction: On a town map grid, the school is at (2, 3) and the park is at (2, 7). Each unit is 1 block.
steps.work: Both locations have x = 2, so they are on the same vertical line.
steps.explanation: Matching x-coordinates means the locations are directly above/below each other.

steps.instruction: Find how far apart they are.
steps.explanation: For vertical distances, subtract the y-coordinates.

steps.instruction: What does this mean in real life?
steps.work: The park is 4 blocks north of the school.
steps.explanation: The grid translates mathematical coordinates into real-world directions and distances.

title: Finding the Area of a Real-World Region on a Grid

steps.instruction: A playground has corners at (1, 2), (6, 2), (6, 5), (1, 5). Each unit is 1 meter.
steps.work: This is a rectangle on the coordinate plane.
steps.explanation: The corners define a rectangle with sides parallel to the axes.

steps.instruction: Calculate length and width from coordinates.
steps.explanation: Subtract the smaller coordinate from the larger for each dimension.
The playground covers 15 square meters of real-world space.
